mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean when FEDFUNDS declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.5%, or signal loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate output and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 210 reflect entrenched inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive future rate hikes, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10 across multiple rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 1.5 percent signals a severe supply-side contraction and immediate recession risk, triggering rapid equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in <40% of instances following the threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample validation.
